options.text: A) Cold and moist skin, high body temperature
B) Cold and dry skin, low body temperature
C) Hot and moist skin, high body temperature

explanation: Heatstroke is a medical emergency characterized by a high body temperature (often above 104°F or 40°C), hot and often dry skin (though it can be moist), and altered mental status. The skin is not typically cold in heatstroke.
